aliases_observed: Merrill Lynch, Pierce, Fenner & Smith Incorporated

deal_flags.reason: Company A approached 11/14/2012 via investment bank with draft NDA proposal; meeting cancelled, no NDA signed, no price, no further interest when contacted again during Apr 2013 outreach. Skipped per §M1.

Summer 2011 / Summer 2012 pre-process lunch meetings between Mr. Moshayedi and WDC executives (Mr. Coyne in 2011; Mr. Hajeck in summer 2012). General/exploratory only, no NDA, no price, no specific transaction terms. Skipped per §M1.

Filing narrates that 'six potential strategic acquirers expressed an interest in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec' — these are partial-company bids not emitted as bid rows per §H3. Company C (3/13/13, asset only), Company F (4/24/13 decline mgmt presentation - select assets), Company E (shortly after 4/24, select assets) are named in this group; three remaining partial-asset buyers are unnamed.

Filing narrates that 'nine prospective acquirers, including the financial sponsor and Company A, indicated they were not interested in a potential transaction.' These parties signed no NDA and gave no price. Skipped per §M1.

events.comments: Board authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to begin exploration of strategic alternatives including a potential sale of the company.
events.source_quote: Consequently, in mid-November, 2012, our board of directors authorized management to contact potential financial advisors to explore their interest in representing the company in its exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the company.

events.comments: Balch Hill Partners, L.P., an activist investor in the technology sector, reported 6.4% ownership; filed first 13D on 11/16/2012; subsequently urged board to explore strategic alternatives.
events.source_quote: Around this timeframe, Balch Hill Partners, L.P. (“Balch Hill”), an activist investor in the technology sector, reported its acquisition of 6.4% of sTec’s outstanding shares. Balch Hill filed its first Schedule 13D with the SEC on November 16, 2012.

events.additional_note: 13D amendment + letter to board urging strategic alternatives
events.comments: Balch Hill filed a Schedule 13D amendment and issued a letter to sTec's board urging it to explore strategic alternatives.
events.source_quote: Then, on December 6, 2012, Balch Hill filed an amendment to its Schedule 13D, and concurrently issued a letter to our board of directors urging our board to reduce costs, focus on products with a SAS interface, and explore strategic alternatives.

events.additional_note: special committee formally established
events.comments: Board established special committee with mandate to conduct a review of sTec's strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company.
events.source_quote: During the meeting on February 13, our board of directors established a special committee of the board, initially composed of Dr. Daly, and Messrs. Colpitts and Witte, and subsequently expanded to four members on March 26, 2013. The mandate of the special committee was to conduct a review of sTec’s strategic alternatives, including a possible sale of the company, as well as the alternative of continuing as a standalone company with further reductions in our workforce and other cost reduction initiatives.

events.comments: Company B, a participant in the electronics industry; President intended to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to Company B's management.
events.source_quote: On February 13, 2013, Mr. Manouch Moshayedi met with the President of Company B, a participant in the electronics industry. Company B’s President stated that the following week Company B was holding a management meeting at which Company B would be discussing its SSD opportunities, and the President intended to present sTec as a possible acquisition target to the management of Company B.

events.additional_note: voluntary; Company B's management declined after review
events.comments: Company B's management determined it was not interested in acquiring sTec; declined voluntarily approximately two weeks after the February 13 approach.
events.source_quote: Approximately two weeks later, the President of Company B contacted Mr. Manouch Moshayedi to inform Mr. Moshayedi that he had shared sTec’s information with Company B’s executive management, but that Company B’s management had determined that it was not interested in acquiring sTec and believed that it could independently develop the technology it needed to compete in enterprise SSDs.

flags.reason: phrase: 'Approximately two weeks later' anchored on 2013-02-13 Company B approach

events.comments: Company D, a participant in the storage industry; head of corporate development contacted management to express interest in exploring a potential acquisition.
events.source_quote: In mid-March, 2013, the head of corporate development for Company D, a participant in the storage industry, contacted our management to express the interest of Company D in exploring a potential acquisition of the company.

events.additional_note: engagement letter signed 3/28/2013
events.comments: Board approved retention of BofA Merrill Lynch as sTec's financial advisor on 3/26/2013; engagement letter signed 3/28/2013.
events.source_quote: At the same meeting and upon the recommendation of the special committee, the board approved retaining BofA Merrill Lynch to act as sTec’s financial advisor in connection with the company’s general review of strategic alternatives and defensive preparedness, subject to final engagement term negotiations by Messrs. Saman and Morales.

events.additional_note: one of 18 prospective acquirers contacted by BofA; later submitted written IOI
events.comments: WDC was among the 18 prospective acquirers BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting on April 1, 2013; WDC ultimately submitted written indications of interest.
events.source_quote: On April 1, 2013, as directed by the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers of sTec. In total, BofA Merrill Lynch had discussions with 18 prospective acquirers, 17 of which were technology companies and one of which was a financial sponsor. Of these, three prospective acquirers (WDC, Company H, a participant in the storage industry, and Company D) submitted written indications of interest for a complete purchase of sTec, six potential strategic acquirers expressed an interest in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and nine prospective acquirers, including the financial sponsor and Company A, indicated they were not interested in a potential transaction.

events.additional_note: includes 'don't ask, don't waive' provision
events.comments: Company E, a participant in the storage industry, signed NDA with don't-ask-don't-waive provision.
events.source_quote: On April 4, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E, a participant in the storage industry, on April 10, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D, on April 11, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F, another

events.comments: Company D executed NDA.
events.source_quote: On April 4, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company E, a participant in the storage industry, on April 10, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company D, on April 11, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company F, another

events.additional_note: includes 'don't ask, don't waive' provision
events.comments: Company F, another participant in the storage industry, signed NDA with don't-ask-don't-waive provision.
events.source_quote: participant in the storage industry, and on April 17, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G, a participant in the storage industry.

events.comments: Company G, a participant in the storage industry, signed NDA.
events.source_quote: participant in the storage industry, and on April 17, it entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company G, a participant in the storage industry. Also on April 17, 2013, sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC.

events.additional_note: addendum to existing NDA predating the current process
events.comments: WDC executed an addendum to its pre-existing NDA with sTec; original NDA date not narrated in filing.
events.source_quote: Also on April 17, 2013, sTec entered into an addendum to its existing non-disclosure agreement with WDC. In total sTec executed six non-disclosure agreements related to the exploration of a potential sale of the company.

events.additional_note: process letters sent to WDC and Company D 4/23 and Company G 4/26; deadline 5/3/2013
events.comments: BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters requesting non-binding indications of interest from WDC and Company D (4/23), and later Company G (4/26); deadline 5/3/2013.
events.source_quote: On April 23, 2013, at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 informal: 'verbal indication' of interest, pre-process-letter deadline (process letters with 5/3 deadline sent same day); single-bound '>$5.60/share' signals non-binding indication; no commitment letters, no merger agreement markup.
events.additional_note: verbal indication during call; single lower-bound
events.comments: Company D verbal indication: interested at a price greater than $5.60 per share.
events.source_quote: Also on April 23, 2013, Messrs. Mark Moshayedi, Morales and Saman, a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch and representatives of Company D held a call to discuss Company D’s interest in a potential transaction and certain diligence requests. On the call, Company D provided a verbal indication of its interest to pursue a transaction at a price greater than $5.60 per share and detailed their additional diligence requirements for submitting a written indication of interest.

flags.reason: single lower bound: 'greater than $5.60 per share'

events.additional_note: voluntarily declined mgmt presentation; only interested in select assets
events.comments: Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation; only interested in select assets. Drop reason: scope mismatch (partial assets only, not whole company).
events.source_quote: In addition, on April 24, Company F declined the invitation to schedule a management presentation, indicating it was only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and Company F had no further communications with BofA Merrill Lynch or sTec regarding a possible transaction.

events.additional_note: only interested in select assets; special committee decided not to continue
events.comments: Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec; special committee decided not to continue discussions.
events.source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Company E indicated it was also only interested in purchasing limited, select assets of sTec, and as a result the special committee decided not to continue discussions with Company E.

flags.reason: phrase: 'Shortly thereafter' anchored on 2013-04-24 Company F decline (7-day offset per §B1 anchored/relative table)

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 informal: 'written indication of interest' in response to non-binding process letter; range bid ($6.60-$7.10) — §G1 range-is-informal structural signal satisfies §G2 via range alternative.
events.comments: WDC first written non-binding indication of interest: range $6.60-$7.10 per share in cash.
events.source_quote: On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.

events.additional_note: voluntary; declined to continue in the process at informal IOI deadline
events.comments: Company G indicated it would not continue in the process on the informal IOI deadline date.
events.source_quote: Also on May 3, 2013, Company G indicated it would not continue in the process.

events.additional_note: informal-round deadline
events.comments: Deadline for non-binding indications of interest (informal round).
events.source_quote: On April 23, 2013, at the direction of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent process letters to WDC and Company D requesting non-binding indications of interest by May 3, 2013.

events.additional_note: includes 'don't ask, don't waive' provision
events.comments: Company H, a participant in the storage industry, signed NDA on 5/8/2013 with don't-ask-don't-waive provision, after contacting BofA 5/1/2013 to express interest.
events.source_quote: Also on May 8, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 informal: explicitly 'written non-binding indication of interest'; draft exclusivity agreement accompanies; no commitment letters, no merger agreement markup, submitted pre-final-round-letter phase.
events.additional_note: accompanied by draft exclusivity agreement
events.comments: Company D written non-binding indication: $5.75 per share in cash, with draft exclusivity agreement.
events.source_quote: Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 informal: explicitly 'written non-binding indication of interest'; range bid ($5.00-$5.75) — §G1 range-is-informal structural signal satisfies §G2 via range alternative.
events.comments: Company H written non-binding indication: range $5.00-$5.75 per share in cash.
events.source_quote: On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.

events.additional_note: final-round process letters + draft merger agreement sent to WDC and Company D, deadline 5/28/2013
events.comments: Final-round process letters and a draft merger agreement sent to WDC and Company D, requesting response by 5/28/2013.
events.source_quote: After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.

flags.reason: Filing uses 'final round process letters' phrase; explicitly a formal final-round invitation with merger-agreement draft.

events.additional_note: unable to increase indicated value range
events.comments: Company H indicated it remained interested but was not able to increase its indicated value range; effectively dropped from active process. Voluntary in the sense that Company H declined to raise price.
events.source_quote: On May 23, 2013, representatives of Company H contacted a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company H remained interested in a potential acquisition of the company but that Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range.

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 formal: 'written second-round indication of interest' submitted in response to final-round process letter with 'mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements'; merger-agreement-markup is a §G1 formal trigger.
events.additional_note: second-round IOI with merger-agreement markup and ancillary agreement drafts; contemplated signing by 6/3/2013
events.comments: WDC second-round IOI: $9.15 per share in cash, with merger-agreement markup and drafts of voting agreement, covenant not to sue, technical services agreement, non-compete, landlord consent.
events.source_quote: On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements, including a voting agreement to be entered into by each of sTec’s officers and directors, a covenant not to sue to be entered into by Mr. Manouch Moshayedi, a technical services agreement to be entered into by Mr. Mark Moshayedi, a non-competition agreement to be entered into by each of Mr. Manouch Moshayedi and Mr. Mark Moshayedi, and a landlord’s consent to assignment to be entered into by an affiliate of Messrs. Moshayedi.

events.additional_note: formal final-round submission deadline
events.comments: Formal final-round deadline; WDC submitted second-round IOI; Company D indicated need for 2 more weeks.
events.source_quote: After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.

events.additional_note: board requested 'best and final' by 5/30/2013
events.comments: Board directed BofA to request 'best and final' proposal from WDC and Company D by 5/30/2013.
events.source_quote: After discussion, our board of directors directed BofA Merrill Lynch to request a “best and final” proposal from WDC and a “best and final” written proposal from Company D by May 30, 2013.

events.additional_note: extended deadline; WDC verbally reconfirmed $9.15 'best and final'; Company D needed more time
events.comments: Extended final-round deadline; WDC verbally indicated $9.15 was best possible; Company D continued to require more time.
events.source_quote: On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC’s indication of interest to acquire the company at a price of $9.15 per share was WDC’s best possible price, and reiterated the concern of WDC regarding the price.

events.additional_note: voluntary; reevaluating interest; discontinued due diligence; temporary withdrawal (later re-engaged 6/10)
events.comments: WDC representatives communicated that WDC was reevaluating its interest and was not prepared to move forward at that time; also discontinued due diligence. Temporary withdrawal before re-engagement on 6/10/2013.
events.source_quote: On May 31, 2013, a representative of WDC communicated to BofA Merrill Lynch that WDC was reevaluating its interest in acquiring sTec, and that WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time. In addition, WDC discontinued their due diligence efforts with the company at that time.

events.additional_note: voluntary; 'would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process'
events.comments: Company D voluntarily disengaged; cited inability to conduct due diligence for more than two weeks.
events.source_quote: On June 5, 2013 representatives of Company D contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company D would not be in a position to actively conduct due diligence for more than two weeks and was disengaging from the process.

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 informal: range bid ($6.60-$7.10) — §G1 range-is-informal structural signal satisfies §G2 via range alternative; filing describes this as a revised 'written indication of interest' after WDC's temporary withdrawal; not described as binding.
events.additional_note: revised IOI after temporary withdrawal; re-engagement after 5/31 drop
events.comments: WDC revised written indication of interest at $6.60-$7.10/share cash range, lowered from 5/28 formal $9.15 bid; submitted after 5/31 temporary withdrawal.
events.source_quote: On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.

flags.reason: bidder WDC previously dropped on 2013-05-31 (temporary withdrawal); re-engaged with revised IOI

events.bid_type_inference_note: §G1 formal: 'best and final price'; WDC proceeding on merger-agreement markup and terms previously proposed on 5/28 (a §G1 formal trigger); definitive point value $6.85 submitted to enable announcement on 6/24/2013.
events.additional_note: best and final; to proceed on transaction terms previously proposed on 5/28/2013; indicated could announce 6/24/2013
events.comments: WDC final written indication of interest: $6.85/share cash, best and final; proceeding on merger-agreement terms of 5/28.
events.source_quote: On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013. In communicating the revised proposal to BofA Merrill Lynch, the WDC representative again stated that WDC was only interested in proceeding with the transaction at a purchase price of $6.85 per share, WDC’s best and final price, for the reasons previously noted, and otherwise on the transaction terms previously proposed on May 28, 2013.

events.additional_note: merger agreement signed 6/23/2013; joint press release 6/24/2013 announced execution
events.comments: sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement on 6/23/2013; joint press release on 6/24/2013 announced execution. All-cash consideration of $6.85/share.
source_quote: On June 23, 2013, sTec, WDC, and Merger Sub executed the merger agreement; each of our officers and directors executed their respective voting agreements with WDC; Mr. Manouch Moshayedi and WDC executed the covenant not to sue, the consulting services agreement and a non-competition agreement; Mr. Mark Moshayedi and WDC executed the technical services agreement and a non-competition agreement; sTec and Messrs. Moshayedi executed the letter agreements; and an affiliate of Messrs. Moshayedi and WDC executed a landlord’s consent to assignment.
On June 24, 2013, prior to the opening of trading on the U.S. stock exchanges, WDC and sTec announced in a joint press release the execution of the merger agreement.

events.comments: Potomac joined Balch Hill as activist investor between 11/16/2012 and 12/06/2012; exact date not narrated in filing.
events.source_quote: Balch Hill was later joined by Potomac Capital Partners II, L.P. (“Potomac”), and Balch Hill and Potomac subsequently jointly increased their beneficial holdings to 9.8%.

flags.reason: filing narrates Potomac joined Balch Hill as co-activist but gives no specific date (only 'later')
null bid_date_precise (date_unknown); placed by narrative position between Balch Hill 11/16/2012 13D and 12/06/2012 amendment
